title: Museums Compete for a Big Prize
summary: Five museums in the UK are finalists for the Art Fund Museum of the Year award, which gives a prize of £120,000. The museums are Norwich Castle Museum and Art Gallery, Fitzwilliam Museum in Cambridge, The Box in Plymouth, The National Gallery in London, and V&A East Storehouse in London. They were chosen because they show great creativity and new ideas. The director of Art Fund, Jenny Waldman, hopes people will visit these museums and their local ones. Norwich Castle reopened after five years of work and now shows life from the 12th century. The Box tells the history of Plymouth. The winner will be announced on June 25 at a ceremony in London, and the other four finalists will each get £20,000. The prize money comes from Art Fund members who buy a National Art Pass and supporters. Last year, Beamish museum won.

background_read: The Art Fund Museum of the Year is a big award for museums in the UK. It helps museums get money to improve and show their collections. Museums are important because they teach us about art, history, and culture. People can visit museums to learn and have fun. This article talks about five museums that are doing great things and might win the prize.
Article_Structure: WHO: Art Fund director Jenny Waldman and five museums in the UK
WHAT: The Art Fund Museum of the Year award with a £120,000 prize
WHERE: Museums in Norwich, Cambridge, Plymouth, and London
WHY: To celebrate creativity and innovation in museums and inspire people to visit them

perspectives.description: The award encourages museums to connect with people and share their collections.
